channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Exposes Shortcomings in U.S. Military Industrial Base
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is taking on a problem that the Pentagon and Congress have tried, and mostly failed, to address for years.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is attempting to take on the shortcomings in the Pentagon’s weapons procurement systems while pushing for a $1.5 trillion budget.

item.title: Trump Says He Postponed a ‘Very Major Attack’ on Iran, Leaving Mideast on Edge
item.description: President Trump threatened “another big hit” on Iran, saying the country had days to return to the negotiating table, as Pakistan continued its mediation efforts to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. military was prepared for a “full, large scale assault” against Iran.

item.title: Top Treasury Lawyer Resigns After Creation of ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’
item.description: Brian Morrissey, the department’s general counsel, stepped down hours after the Trump administration announced the $1.8 billion fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Treasury Department’s general counsel, Brian Morrissey, resigned on Monday, three people familiar with the move said.

item.title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
item.description: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow and Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson of the Supreme Court talks with Judge Richard Gergel of the U.S. District Court in South Carolina during a fireside chat hosted by the American Law Institute in Washington on Monday.

item.title: Before the San Diego Mosque Shooting, a Fearful Mother’s Phone Call Set Off a Frantic Search
item.description: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Police officers raced around San Diego before converging at the mosque.

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting: What to Know About the Suspects and What Happened
item.description: Three men were killed in the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Two teenage suspects were found dead in a car nearby, police said.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Families escorting their children from the Islamic Center in San Diego, after a deadly shooting on Monday.

item.title: G.O.P. Supporters Back Trump, but a Third Seek a New Direction for the Party
item.description: While President Trump remains overwhelmingly popular within the Republican coalition, a New York Times/Siena poll found, a sizable share wants the party’s next nominee to take a different approach.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Karl Russell

item.title: Trump’s Push to Defeat Thomas Massie Is the Most Expensive House Primary in Recent Years
item.description: The Republican primary in Representative Thomas Massie’s district has drawn vast spending, but it is just one of several contests on Tuesday that will test the power of big money.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Matt Zdun
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times

item.title: A Single Donation Could Upend the G.O.P. Primary for Texas Attorney General
item.description: One donor’s contribution of $2.75 million could help propel Representative Chip Roy in his clash with a self-funded state senator, Mayes Middleton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

item.title: How Trump Entrusted His World Cup to Another Giuliani
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the son of Rudy and a longtime Trump loyalist, is overseeing American hosting duties for the World Cup next month. It’s a big promotion.
item.dc:creator: Matt Flegenheimer and Rebecca R. Ruiz
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Future of Truth’ Contains Quotes Made Up by A.I.
item.description: Steven Rosenbaum, author of “The Future of Truth,” said he had started his own investigation after The New York Times asked about the fake quotes.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s ChatGPT on a laptop. The rise of A.I. has set off fears among publishers that they may accidentally release books from authors who improperly use A.I.-generated language.

item.title: China Wants A.I. to Flourish, but Not at the Expense of Jobs
item.description: A series of precedent-setting rulings signals that Chinese courts are being enlisted to shield workers from displacement by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A robot learns to unpack and fold boxes at a lab working with Meituan in Shenzhen in January.

item.title: On Blind, Anxious Tech Workers Get the Lowdown on Layoffs
item.description: On the website Blind, professionals share advice — and gallows humor — anonymously. It is chronicling the curdling of tech optimism.
item.dc:creator: Noam Scheiber
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sunguk Moon, a founder and the chief executive of Blind, said sentiment on the site “went from personal career planning to mass anxiety.”

item.title: As a Weakened Putin Follows Trump to Beijing, Iran War Offers an Opening
item.description: With the upheaval in the Persian Gulf disrupting oil and gas supplies, Russia is looking to deepen its energy ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Alexander Kazakov/Sputnik, via Reuters
item.media:description: A photo released by Russian state media shows Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing, last year.

item.title: As Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Sites, an Environmental Disaster Unfolds
item.description: After invading more than four years ago, Moscow has usually been the one causing ecological disaster. But Kyiv’s strikes, intended to cut into the Kremlin’s oil revenue, have flipped the script.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva and Alina Lobzina
item.media:credit: Vantor, via Reuters
item.media:description: A satellite image from the aerial mapping company Vantor of a Russian oil facility in Tuapse, Russia, after Ukrainian drone attacks in April.

item.title: W.H.O. Chief Is ‘Deeply Concerned’ by Speed and Scale of Ebola Outbreak
item.description: Health officials reported more than 130 suspected deaths and 513 cases, a sharp rise since the outbreak was first identified in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang and Lynsey Chutel
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Jospin Mwisha/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A visitor has their temperature checked before entering Kyeshero Hospital in Goma on Monday.

item.title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak as Cases and Deaths Rise
item.description: More than 130 deaths and over 500 cases are suspected. The United States restricted entry for people who have been to three African countries after a global health emergency was declared.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Jospin Mwisha/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Outside a hospital in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on Sunday.

item.title: ‘We Don’t Have a Choice’: Tough Commutes Persist After L.I.R.R. Strike Deal
item.description: Transit officials and unions representing Long Island Rail Road employees agreed to a new contract on Monday, ending a three-day strike. But service remained limited on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anusha Bayya, Miles G. Cohen and Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Long Island Rail Road service was suspended on Tuesday morning, even after a deal was reached to end a strike by rail workers.

item.title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
item.description: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Gabriela Bhaskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Screens on either side of a Tesla Semi’s steering wheel provide a view of the traffic around the vehicle.

item.title: Amazon E-Bikes Take Root in Manhattan and Brooklyn
item.description: Small companies that handle the final delivery are relying on them to skirt New York City traffic. Amazon plans to try the bikes elsewhere, too.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Vincent Alban
item.media:description: Brandon Quizeno making Amazon deliveries with an e-bike in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: The Best of ‘S.N.L.’ Season 51: Sensitive Strippers and Regretful Moms
item.description: This season “Saturday Night Live” found new ways to satirize the Trump administration and said goodbye to one of its most valuable cast members.
item.dc:creator: Dave Itzkoff
item.media:credit: Will Heath/NBC, via Getty Images
item.media:description: In a memorable “Saturday Night Live” sketch this season, a woman (Ashley Padilla, with Tommy Brennan and Jane Wickline) reconsidered her support for President Trump.

item.title: Is There a Door No. 3 for Democrats?
item.description: Is there anything Democrats can do to break free of a deeply polarized political system in which parties are constantly winning and then losing office?
item.dc:creator: Thomas B. Edsall
item.media:credit: Joshua Dudley Greer for The New York Times

item.title: The Race to Control Congress
item.description: We look at where the parties stand leading up to the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie campaigning in Kentucky last week.

item.title: Jon Stewart Asks What ‘Daddy’ Brought Back From China
item.description: “He was gone for a couple of days in China. We were scared,” Stewart said of President Trump on Monday’s “The Daily Show.” “But now he’s home, and I’m sure he brought us all the goodies.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
item.description: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Tristan Spinski for The New York Times
item.media:description: NextEra Energy’s acquisition of Dominion Energy would consolidate utility operations along much of the East Coast, which could shape electricity service for tens of millions of customers.

item.title: Activity Slows in Sandy Fire in Simi Valley. Evacuation Orders Remain.
item.description: More than 33,000 people remain under evacuation orders after a brush fire broke out in Simi Valley, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Amy Graff
item.media:credit: Ethan Swope/Associated Press
item.media:description: The blaze, called the Sandy fire, broke out on a warm, breezy day in Simi Valley.

item.title: Military Bases Are Rife With ‘Forever Chemicals.’ New Mexico Wants Them Cleaned Up.
item.description: The state is leading the country’s reckoning with PFAS. The outcome of its suit against the federal government will affect how courts treat more than 15,000 other claims nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan and Nina Riggio
item.media:description: Art Schaap, right, a dairy farmer in Clovis, N.M., and James Kenney, of the New Mexico Environment Department, stand in the area where Mr. Schaap had to kill his cattle.

item.title: Dallas World Cup Mural: Artist Wyland Says Beloved Whale Artwork Was Painted Over
item.description: The artwork’s removal ahead of the World Cup has ignited a backlash over public art and what cities are willing to sacrifice for global events.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez
item.media:credit: Julio Cortez/Associated Press
item.media:description: The mural “Ocean Life” in downtown Dallas that is being painted over in preparation for the World Cup was dedicated in 1999.
